name: JEE Main — Joint Entrance Examination (Engineering)
summary: National entrance exam for NITs, IIITs, CFTIs (central technical colleges), and gateway to JEE Advanced for IITs — two sessions per year, offered in 13 languages including Odia.
deadline_pattern: Session 1 registration typically November–December; Session 2 registration February–March. Exam in January and April. Verify at jeemain.nta.nic.in — dates change each year.
documents: Category certificate (if applying under reserved category)
notes: Available in Odia — choosing Odia language ties you to exam centres within Odisha. Odia candidates get a bilingual (Odia + English) booklet; English prevails in case of translation ambiguity. Class 12 with Physics, Chemistry, and Mathematics (PCM) required. Free coaching available: Chhatra Protsahan Yojana (CPY) for ST/SC — ~7 Centres of Excellence; Central Free Coaching Scheme for SC/OBC (coaching.dosje.gov.in). Honest context: highly competitive; prepare for 1–2 years of dedicated study.

name: NEET-UG — National Eligibility cum Entrance Test (Medical)
odia_name: NEET-UG — ଜାତୀୟ ଯୋଗ୍ୟତା ଓ ପ୍ରବେଶ ପରୀକ୍ଷା (ଚିକିତ୍ସା)
hindi_name: NEET-UG — राष्ट्रीय पात्रता सह प्रवेश परीक्षा (मेडिकल)
summary: Single national entrance exam for MBBS, BDS, AYUSH, nursing, and allied health UG courses — once a year, pen-paper exam, available in 13 languages including Odia.
deadline_pattern: Registration typically February–March; exam in May. Verify at neet.nta.nic.in — dates change each year.
documents: Class 12 marksheet with Physics, Chemistry, Biology (PCB)
notes: Requires Class 12 PCB (Physics, Chemistry, Biology). Offered in Odia: bilingual (Odia + English) booklet; English prevails in dispute. Odia language selection ties candidate to Odisha exam centres. For those who cannot afford coaching: CPY free coaching (ST/SC, stsc.odisha.gov.in); OPTCL/PSU CSR free residential NEET coaching at Aakash Institute Bhubaneswar (EWS — verify availability). GNM nursing is also accessible via DMET Odisha and does NOT require NEET.

name: CUET-UG — Common University Entrance Test (Central & Participating Universities)
summary: Common entrance test for admissions to all central universities and many state/private participating universities across India — available in 13 languages including Odia, no upper age limit, Class 12 pass required.
deadline_pattern: Registration typically February–April; exam May–June. Verify at cuet.nta.nic.in.
documents: Class 12 marksheet or admit card (for appearing students)
notes: Offered in Odia. Minimum qualifying marks for admission vary by institution and course: typically 50% for General/OBC, 40% for SC/ST/PwD at central universities — verify per institution in the current CUET information bulletin. No upper age limit (unlike some other exams). Central Universities of Odisha: Utkal University, Berhampur University, Sambalpur University (state), and Ravenshaw University participate or may participate — verify the current list on cuet.nta.nic.in.

name: OJEE — Odisha Joint Entrance Examination
summary: Odisha state-level exam covering lateral entry into B.Tech 2nd year (for ITI/diploma holders), B.Pharm, MCA, MBA, M.Tech, integrated MBA, and some nursing courses — the main route for ITI/polytechnic graduates to enter B.Tech without JEE.
deadline_pattern: Registration typically March–April; verify at ojee.nic.in.
documents: ITI certificate or Diploma marksheet (for lateral entry)
Class 12 marksheet (for direct B.Pharm / non-lateral entry courses)
notes: Fresh B.Tech admissions are now largely via JEE Main rank (not OJEE). OJEE focuses on lateral entry and PG/pharmacy/professional. Key reservation facts: SC/ST get 5% relaxation in minimum aggregate; 30% reservation for women in B.Tech/B.Plan/B.Arch/MBA/M.Tech (verify current brochure). OL (Outlying Odia) category for Odia-speakers residing outside Odisha (requires nativity certificate + Odia language test). ITI holders fill 80% of B.Tech lateral entry seats — 20% go to +2 PCM students. This is the 'second door to engineering' for ITI/polytechnic students who did not take JEE.
